outcome.note: No edit was generated for this iteration.

lesson: Dense progress reward (r_approach_region) still dominates, preventing stable landings despite previous moderate weakening. Coupled reduction of r_approach_region by 50% and further increase of terminal bonuses (r_stable_landing_once to 180, r_safe_contact_once to 80) follows a proven pattern to shift the attractor from hovering to landing.

metadata.note: Aligned initial schema for EG-RSA. v1 adds semantic_role metadata so generic tools can reason over reward roles rather than environment-specific names.

validation_errors: Tool audit flagged edit_plan; requesting Agent repair instead of direct execution.
Repaired edit_plan also failed tool audit; using continue_training.

warnings: Low step evidence for target r_stable_landing_once: ratio=0.0000, trigger_rate=0.0000; warning only.
Low step evidence for target r_safe_contact_once: ratio=0.0000, trigger_rate=0.0000; warning only.

reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Reducing the dominant dense component alone may degrade progress toward the pad, while increasing terminal bonus alone may not overcome the strong dense local optimum. Both must be applied together to shift the attractor from hovering to stable landing.
editor_constraints: Target r_approach_region for weight reduction (factor 0.5 recommended, i.e., weight 0.525)
Pair any reduction with an increase in r_stable_landing_once weight (factor 1.8 recommended, target 180)
Optionally increase r_safe_contact_once weight to 80-100 to provide intermediate progress incentive
Do NOT alter r_landing_quality, r_stability, or r_approach_progress; they are working as needed.
Keep the one-time nature of event rewards intact.
Preserve semantic roles and existing structure.
Consider adding a small penalty component for contact state toggling (structural search) if hovering persists after this edit.
must_preserve: All stability components (r_stability, r_landing_quality) at current weights
The existing dense progress components except r_approach_region (r_approach_progress, etc.)
must_avoid: Increasing terminal bonus beyond 250 in a single step
Reducing or removing stability quality components
Removing all dense guidance, which might collapse exploration
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: Stable landing success rate should improve above 0%, episode length decrease, contact toggles reduce. The dominant reward component will shift from r_approach_region toward terminal events.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: If r_approach_region is reduced too aggressively, the agent may lose incentive to approach the pad at all, leading to even lower progress. If terminal bonus becomes too large, agent might discover an unstable bounce that triggers the bonus without robust landing. Conservative adjustment within memory-tested bounds mitigates these risks.

reflection_report.reflection_summary: Agent's performance regressed with the previously effective schema. Dense approach_region reward dominates again, causing hovering near the pad without stable landing. Terminal bonuses never trigger. Unstable contact behavior persists. The harmful lesson indicates reducing stability components while pushing terminal is risky, but further reduction of the dense guidance component and increased terminal bonus is needed.

observed_facts: Long episodes (mean 541.8 steps) with high contact toggles (mean 137.5)
Hack score 0.4, repeated_event_exploitation and shaping_goal_mismatch flags raised
likely_true_failures: Dense progress guidance (r_approach_region) overweighted, creating local optimum of hovering
Terminal bonus (100) insufficient to overcome loss of dense reward during final descent
Agent may be attempting to trigger terminal event but cannot maintain stable contact, leading to repeated toggling
likely_false_positives: Repeated_event_exploitation flag: likely due to unstable hovering rather than deliberate exploitation, as one-time bonuses are not triggered.
root_cause_hypotheses: Overweighted dense progress reward relative to sparse terminal success causes agent to prefer hovering near pad
Terminal bonus magnitude too low to shift attractor from dense reward local optimum

reusable_lessons: lesson_iter_000_to_001: coupled reduction of dominant dense component (r_approach_region) and increase of terminal bonus (r_stable_landing_once) shifted agent from hovering to landing. Applicable again with further reduction of r_approach_region.
failed_or_weak_lessons: lesson_iter_001_to_002: reducing stability quality (r_landing_quality) while increasing terminal caused regression. Avoid touching stability components.
avoid_actions: Do not decrease r_landing_quality or r_stability
Do not increase terminal bonus alone without reducing dominant dense component
Do not increase terminal bonus above 250 in one step
Do not remove all dense guidance
recommended_actions: Consider increasing r_safe_contact_once weight (to 80-100) to bridge safe contact
Optionally add a penalty for contact toggling to discourage hovering (structural search)

package_should_be_rejected_if: It reduces any stability component (r_stability, r_landing_quality)
It removes or disables r_approach_region without a coupled terminal increase
It introduces a dense reward for being in landing region that can be trivially exploited
package_should_be_accepted_if: It reduces r_approach_region weight by factor ≤ 0.7 (i.e., at least 30% reduction)
It increases r_stable_landing_once weight to at least 150
It optionally increases r_safe_contact_once weight to at least 80
It keeps the edit atomic, not splitting dense/terminal adjustments
safety_notes: Monitor training to ensure success rate trends upward and not just a spike

Watch for unstable bounce-and-land exploits: if agent fires main engine just before contact to trigger 5-step stable then crashes, consider increasing required stable steps or imposing a post-landing stability check.

outcome_decision.reason: True semantic hack risk increased: repeated reward payment, high-reward/low-progress, or shaping-goal mismatch.

observed_facts: 0% landing success, 0% terminal reward payment across episodes
Dominant component r_approach_region (40.8% of total reward)
Long episodes (mean 541.8 steps) with high contact toggles (mean 137.5, max 338)
Progress score mean 0.466, shaping_goal_mismatch flag true
Hack score 0.4, repeated_event_exploitation and shaping_goal_mismatch flags raised
likely_true_failures: Dense progress guidance (r_approach_region) overweighted, creating a local optimum of hovering near the pad without stable landing
Terminal bonus (100) insufficient to overcome loss of dense reward during final descent
Unstable contact toggling likely from agent attempting but failing to maintain stable contact
likely_false_positives: Repeated_event_exploitation flag: likely due to unstable hovering rather than deliberate exploitation, as one-time bonuses are not actually triggered
root_cause_hypotheses: Overweighted dense progress reward relative to sparse terminal success causes agent to prefer hovering near the pad
Terminal bonus magnitude too low to shift the attractor from the dense reward local optimum

lesson_assessments.reason: Successful coupled rebalancing from iteration 0: reduced dominant dense guidance (r_approach_region) and increased terminal bonuses when terminal never triggered. Current situation parallels that exactly with dominant r_approach_region and untriggered terminal bonuses. This lesson is highly applicable.
Reducing stability quality (r_landing_quality) while increasing terminal caused severe regression. Must avoid repeating this pattern.

avoid_actions: Do not decrease r_landing_quality or r_stability (stability quality components)
Do not increase terminal bonus alone without reducing the dominant dense component
Do not increase terminal bonus above 250 in one step
Do not remove all dense guidance
recommended_actions: Optionally increase r_safe_contact_once weight to 80–100 to provide an intermediate bridging incentive

reward_editor.rationale: The dense approach_region component dominates again (40.8% of total reward), and terminal bonuses never trigger, leading to hovering and shaping-goal mismatch. Following the successful lesson from iter_000_to_001, a coupled rebalancing reducing r_approach_region weight and increasing terminal bonus weights is appropriate. Further reduction of r_approach_region by factor 0.5 (to 0.525) and increase of r_stable_landing_once by factor 1.8 (to 180) will shift the attractor toward stable landing. Optional increase of r_safe_contact_once to 80 (factor 1.6) provides an intermediate sparse incentive. This atomic package avoids touching stability components, which caused regression previously. The edit is conservative within memory-tested bounds and aligns with auditor guidelines.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Stable landing success rate should improve above 0%; episode length and contact toggles should decrease; the dominant reward component will shift from r_approach_region toward terminal events.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: If r_approach_region is reduced too aggressively, the agent may lose incentive to approach the pad, leading to even lower progress. If terminal bonus becomes too large, agent might discover an unstable bounce that triggers the bonus without robust landing. The chosen factors (0.5 reduction, 180 terminal) are moderate and keep the balance safe as per auditor hints. The optional increase of r_safe_contact_once is small and provides bridging incentive without risk of dominance.

risks.reason: dense_guidance is reduced while terminal_success pressure is increased before success is reliable.
risks.repair_instruction: Use a milder dense_guidance reduction or keep guidance until success evidence improves.

risks.reason: The proposed edit touches semantic roles associated with a retrieved regression lesson.
risks.repair_instruction: RepairAgent should explain why this role-level pattern will not repeat the prior regression.

behavior_risk_audit.repair_summary: Multiple medium risks under weak success; repair or continue_training is safer than direct execution.

risks.reason: The proposed edit touches semantic roles associated with a retrieved regression lesson.
risks.repair_instruction: RepairAgent should explain why this role-level pattern will not repeat the prior regression.

repair_behavior_risk_audit.repair_summary: Multiple medium risks under weak success; repair or continue_training is safer than direct execution.

what_worked: Coupled reduction of dominant dense component and increase of terminal bonuses (iter_000_to_001): reduced r_approach_region, increased r_stable_landing_once and r_safe_contact_once shifted agent from hovering to landing.
what_failed: Reducing stability quality components (r_landing_quality) while increasing terminal bonus (iter_001_to_002): caused regression, loss of success, and increased hack risk.
avoid_next: Solo decrease_weight on dense components without a paired terminal increase.
Increasing terminal bonus beyond 250 in a single edit.
Removing all dense guidance, which may collapse exploration.
recommend_next: If landing rate remains low after this edit, consider a small contact toggle penalty (structural search) or further increase terminal bonus in small steps, monitoring for exploits.
Always keep edits atomic and preserve stability components.
distilled_lessons.applicability_notes: Coupled rebalancing is effective when a dense component dominates and prevents terminal sparse reward from being earned, especially when the failure is task_failure rather than reward hacking. Stability components should not be reduced during terminal pushes.
